#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via skopeo
# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
#
# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: inspect upstream, measure the canonical
# digest, copy --all into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the member
# tags by GAR->GAR retag (dedups blobs). Author the provenance envelope and stage
# it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
# /builder/outputs/output. Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server.
#
# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="

# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA) — shared library snippet.
#@rbgjs_include token-fetch

# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"

# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
RESULT='{'
FIRST=true

for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
  case "${SLOT}" in
    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"

  # Inspect upstream, take the canonical digest, derive the glance fingerprint —
  # shared library snippet (requires ORIGIN + RAW_FILE; provides SHA + FINGERPRINT).
  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
#@rbgjs_include skopeo-fingerprint

  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"

  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT}"

  # Collision guard — never silently clobber an existing Lode. The bole handle
  # (:rbi_bole) always points at this touchmark's captured image, so inspect it:
  # absent => a fresh touchmark; present => re-use. Re-use is legitimate ONLY when
  # it is the identical canonical digest (a Cloud Build retry re-copying the same
  # bytes); a different digest under the same touchmark is a clobber and fails loud.
  # The check sits immediately before the copy in this same step — atomic with the
  # GAR write in the sense the spec requires (no host pre-submit window). EXISTING_SHA
  # is computed the same way as SHA (sha256sum of the raw manifest), so the compare is
  # apples-to-apples. An inspect failure is treated as absent: cloud-side auth/network
  # is reliable, and a genuine GAR outage fails the copy below regardless.
  EXISTING_RAW="/workspace/ensconce_existing_${SLOT}.json"
  if skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${PKG}:${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" \
       --creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" > "${EXISTING_RAW}" 2>/dev/null; then
    EXISTING_SHA=$(sha256sum "${EXISTING_RAW}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
    if [ "${EXISTING_SHA}" = "${SHA}" ]; then
      echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} already holds identical digest sha256:${SHA} — idempotent retry, proceeding."
    else
      echo "FATAL: touchmark collision at ${PKG}" >&2
      echo "  existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE} -> sha256:${EXISTING_SHA}" >&2
      echo "  refusing to clobber with  -> sha256:${SHA}" >&2
      echo "  (banish the Lode first, or ensconce under a fresh touchmark)" >&2
      exit 1
    fi
  else
    echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} is fresh (no existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE})."
  fi

  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag.
  skopeo copy --all \
    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
    "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Apply remaining member tags by GAR->GAR retag (same blobs, manifest re-tag only).
  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT}"; do
    skopeo copy --all \
      "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
      "docker://${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}" \
      --src-creds  "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
      --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
      || { echo "FATAL: skopeo retag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
  done

  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"

  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
  ENVELOPE='{'
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"

  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
    FIRST=false
  else
    RESULT="${RESULT},"
  fi
  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
done

RESULT="${RESULT}}"

echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 02: Push each Lode's provenance envelope as its :rbi_vouch artifact
3	# Builder: docker (from reliquary)
4	# Entrypoint: bash
5	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH
6	#
7	# Note: The Dockerfile echo-sequence below is intentional — this script runs
8	# inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module discipline.
9	#
10	# Step 01 (skopeo) staged one envelope per captured Lode at
11	# /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json and listed the stamps in
12	# /workspace/lode_stamps.txt. Each envelope rides into the SAME package as the
13	# base manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest, one per Lode.
14	# Vouch content is architecture-independent; single-platform is sufficient.
15	
16	set -euo pipefail
17	
18	echo "=== Assemble and push Lode vouch artifacts ==="
19	
20	test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
21	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
22	test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
23	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }
24	
25	test -n "${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH missing" >&2; exit 1; }
26	
27	# Ensure the shared buildx builder — shared library snippet (run once).
28	#@rbgjs_include buildx-bootstrap
29	
30	while IFS= read -r STAMP || test -n "${STAMP}"; do
31	  test -n "${STAMP}" || continue
32	
33	  ENVELOPE_FILE="/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
34	  test -f "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" \
35	    || { echo "FATAL: envelope not staged for ${STAMP}: ${ENVELOPE_FILE}" >&2; exit 1; }
36	
37	  VOUCH_URI="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}:${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}"
38	  echo "--- Vouch for ${STAMP} -> ${VOUCH_URI} ---"
39	
40	  CTX="/workspace/vouch_ctx_${STAMP}"
41	  mkdir -p "${CTX}"
42	  cp "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" "${CTX}/vouch.json"
43	  echo "FROM scratch"        >  "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
44	  echo "COPY vouch.json /"   >> "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
45	
46	  # Push the FROM-scratch vouch context — shared library snippet.
47	  PUSH_URI="${VOUCH_URI}"
48	  PUSH_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64"
49	  PUSH_CTX="${CTX}"
50	#@rbgjs_include buildx-push
51	
52	  echo "Vouch pushed: ${VOUCH_URI}"
53	done < /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
54	
55	echo "=== Vouch push step complete ==="
56

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 02: Push each Lode's provenance envelope as its :rbi_vouch artifact
# Builder: docker (from reliquary)
# Entrypoint: bash
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH
#
# Note: The Dockerfile echo-sequence below is intentional — this script runs
# inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module discipline.
#
# Step 01 (skopeo) staged one envelope per captured Lode at
# /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json and listed the stamps in
# /workspace/lode_stamps.txt. Each envelope rides into the SAME package as the
# base manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest, one per Lode.
# Vouch content is architecture-independent; single-platform is sufficient.

set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Assemble and push Lode vouch artifacts ==="

test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }

test -n "${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH missing" >&2; exit 1; }

# Ensure the shared buildx builder — shared library snippet (run once).
#@rbgjs_include buildx-bootstrap

while IFS= read -r STAMP || test -n "${STAMP}"; do
  test -n "${STAMP}" || continue

  ENVELOPE_FILE="/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
  test -f "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: envelope not staged for ${STAMP}: ${ENVELOPE_FILE}" >&2; exit 1; }

  VOUCH_URI="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}:${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}"
  echo "--- Vouch for ${STAMP} -> ${VOUCH_URI} ---"

  CTX="/workspace/vouch_ctx_${STAMP}"
  mkdir -p "${CTX}"
  cp "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" "${CTX}/vouch.json"
  echo "FROM scratch"        >  "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
  echo "COPY vouch.json /"   >> "${CTX}/Dockerfile"

  # Push the FROM-scratch vouch context — shared library snippet.
  PUSH_URI="${VOUCH_URI}"
  PUSH_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64"
  PUSH_CTX="${CTX}"
#@rbgjs_include buildx-push

  echo "Vouch pushed: ${VOUCH_URI}"
done < /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

echo "=== Vouch push step complete ==="

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - bole body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
20	#   ensconce — capture an upstream base image into a Lode (Director credentials)
21	# The bole rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
22	# kind-specific data — the ensconce recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch push),
23	# the substitutions blob, and the touchmark-fact extract — and composes them
24	# through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
25	# step-composition machinery lives here.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	# Ensconce is capture-pure: it writes no consumer config. It hands the captured
30	# touchmark to a later derived-pull election (the conjure ANCHOR populator)
31	# through two bare single-form chaining facts (RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK +
32	# RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND) via the depth-1 cross-tabtarget chain. The provenance
33	# envelope lives only in GAR (:rbi_vouch tag, pushed cloud-side by rbgjl02),
34	# never host-side.
35	
36	######################################################################
37	# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)
38	
39	# Internal: compose the ensconce capture recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch
40	# push) and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit and
41	# poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
42	# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
43	# enshrine-borrowed poll ceiling.
44	# Args: token origin stamp
45	zrbld_ensconce_submit() {
46	  zrbld_sentinel
47	
48	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
49	  local -r z_origin="${2:?Origin required}"
50	  local -r z_stamp="${3:?Stamp required}"
51	
52	  buc_step "Constructing ensconce capture recipe"
53	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
54	  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
55	
56	  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
57	  # spine. The | delimiter is load-bearing — builder refs carry colons (tags).
58	  local -r z_recipe=(
59	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}|ensconce-capture|bash"
60	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|assemble-push-vouch|bash"
61	  )
62	
63	  buc_log_args "Composing ensconce substitutions blob"
64	  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}subs.json"
65	  jq -n \
66	    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
67	    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
68	    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
69	    --arg zjq_tag_bole     "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" \
70	    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
71	    --arg zjq_tag_digest   "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" \
72	    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
73	    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
74	    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
75	    --arg zjq_origin_1     "${z_origin}" \
76	    --arg zjq_stamp_1      "${z_stamp}" \
77	    '{
78	      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:          $zjq_gar_host,
79	      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:          $zjq_gar_path,
80	      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:        $zjq_lodes_root,
81	      _RBGL_TAG_BOLE:          $zjq_tag_bole,
82	      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:         $zjq_tag_vouch,
83	      _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX: $zjq_tag_digest,
84	      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:       $zjq_trust_grade,
85	      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:      $zjq_vouch_schema,
86	      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:       $zjq_acquired_by,
87	      _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:    $zjq_origin_1,
88	      _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:    "",
89	      _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:    "",
90	      _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp_1,
91	      _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP:      "",
92	      _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP:      ""
93	    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
94	    || buc_die "Failed to compose ensconce substitutions blob"
95	
96	  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
97	    "${z_token}" "Ensconce" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_ENSHRINE}" \
98	    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}" \
99	    "${z_recipe[@]}"
100	}
101	
102	# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed ensconce build and
103	# emit the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand
104	# enum). The skopeo capture step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the
105	# host-minted stamp per slot; the docker vouch step writes no output. Base-kind
106	# ensconce captures exactly one base, so exactly one slot is populated — the
107	# single-form facts are one-per-dispatch and buf_write_fact_single's no-clobber
108	# guard turns any second populated slot into a loud failure. The provenance
109	# envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR (rbgjl02 pushed it under
110	# :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the touchmark a consumer needs.
111	zrbld_ensconce_extract() {
112	  zrbld_sentinel
113	
114	  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"
115	
116	  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}output.json"
117	  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"
118	
119	  buc_log_args "Ensconce output:"
120	  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"
121	
122	  local z_n=""
123	  local z_slot_key=""
124	  local z_stamp_file=""
125	  local z_stamp=""
126	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
127	    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
128	    z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_stamp.txt"
129	    jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.stamp // empty" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
130	      || buc_die "Failed to read stamp for ${z_slot_key}"
131	    z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
132	    test -n "${z_stamp}" || continue
133	
134	    buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
135	      || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
136	    buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE}" \
137	      || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"
138	    buc_success "Ensconced Lode ${z_stamp} — touchmark fact emitted (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE})"
139	  done
140	}
141	
142	######################################################################
143	# External Functions (rbld_*)
144	
145	rbld_ensconce() {
146	  zrbld_sentinel
147	
148	  buc_doc_brief "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (parallel rbi_ld capture)"
149	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory declaring the base ORIGIN"
150	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
151	
152	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load.
153	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
154	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
155	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
156	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
157	
158	  # Resolve the single base ORIGIN. Base-kind ensconce captures one base per
159	  # Lode per invocation; multi-base vessels are dispatched per base. Every real
160	  # vessel declares exactly one base slot today.
161	  local z_origin=""
162	  local z_origin_count=0
163	  local z_n=""
164	  local z_origin_var=""
165	  local z_slot_origin=""
166	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
167	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
168	    z_slot_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
169	    test -n "${z_slot_origin}" || continue
170	    z_origin="${z_slot_origin}"
171	    z_origin_count=$((z_origin_count + 1))
172	  done
173	
174	  test "${z_origin_count}" -ne 0 \
175	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slot (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
176	  test "${z_origin_count}" -eq 1 \
177	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares ${z_origin_count} base slots; base-kind ensconce captures one base per Lode — invoke per base"
178	
179	  # Reject producer-vessel pins: an origin naming a local vessel directory is a
180	  # made-side hallmark-pin (bind/airgap), not an upstream base to capture.
181	  test ! -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}" \
182	    || buc_die "Origin '${z_origin}' names a producer vessel — base-Lode capture is for upstream bases, not hallmark-pins"
183	
184	  buc_info "Ensconce base: ${z_origin}"
185	
186	  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (skopeo for capture, docker for vouch).
187	  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images
188	
189	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
190	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
191	
192	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
193	  local z_token=""
194	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
195	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
196	
197	  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
198	  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
199	  local z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
200	
201	  # Tweak override: test infrastructure pins the stamp via the bure tweak channel
202	  # to drive two captures onto one touchmark (the collision-guard exercise) — the
203	  # time-based mint is seconds-grained, so two CLI ensconces never share a
204	  # touchmark and the cloud guard's idempotent/collision branches never fire
205	  # without a pin. The name carries the buo sprue (BURE enforces the shape).
206	  # Mirror: rbtdrc_crucible.rs RBTDRC_ENSCONCE_STAMP_TWEAK_NAME — same literal.
207	  local -r z_ensconce_stamp_tweak_name="buorb_ensconce_stamp"
208	  test "${BURE_TWEAK_NAME:-}" != "${z_ensconce_stamp_tweak_name}" || z_stamp="${BURE_TWEAK_VALUE}"
209	
210	  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
211	
212	  zrbld_ensconce_submit "${z_token}" "${z_origin}" "${z_stamp}"
213	  zrbld_ensconce_extract
214	
215	  buc_success "Ensconce complete: ${z_origin} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
216	}
217	
218	# eof
219

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - bole body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
#   ensconce — capture an upstream base image into a Lode (Director credentials)
# The bole rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
# kind-specific data — the ensconce recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch push),
# the substitutions blob, and the touchmark-fact extract — and composes them
# through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
# step-composition machinery lives here.

set -euo pipefail

# Ensconce is capture-pure: it writes no consumer config. It hands the captured
# touchmark to a later derived-pull election (the conjure ANCHOR populator)
# through two bare single-form chaining facts (RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK +
# RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND) via the depth-1 cross-tabtarget chain. The provenance
# envelope lives only in GAR (:rbi_vouch tag, pushed cloud-side by rbgjl02),
# never host-side.

######################################################################
# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)

# Internal: compose the ensconce capture recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch
# push) and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit and
# poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
# enshrine-borrowed poll ceiling.
# Args: token origin stamp
zrbld_ensconce_submit() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
  local -r z_origin="${2:?Origin required}"
  local -r z_stamp="${3:?Stamp required}"

  buc_step "Constructing ensconce capture recipe"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
  # spine. The | delimiter is load-bearing — builder refs carry colons (tags).
  local -r z_recipe=(
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}|ensconce-capture|bash"
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|assemble-push-vouch|bash"
  )

  buc_log_args "Composing ensconce substitutions blob"
  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}subs.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_bole     "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_digest   "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" \
    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
    --arg zjq_origin_1     "${z_origin}" \
    --arg zjq_stamp_1      "${z_stamp}" \
    '{
      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:          $zjq_gar_host,
      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:          $zjq_gar_path,
      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:        $zjq_lodes_root,
      _RBGL_TAG_BOLE:          $zjq_tag_bole,
      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:         $zjq_tag_vouch,
      _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX: $zjq_tag_digest,
      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:       $zjq_trust_grade,
      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:      $zjq_vouch_schema,
      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:       $zjq_acquired_by,
      _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:    $zjq_origin_1,
      _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:    "",
      _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:    "",
      _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp_1,
      _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP:      "",
      _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP:      ""
    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose ensconce substitutions blob"

  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
    "${z_token}" "Ensconce" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_ENSHRINE}" \
    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}" \
    "${z_recipe[@]}"
}

# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed ensconce build and
# emit the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand
# enum). The skopeo capture step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the
# host-minted stamp per slot; the docker vouch step writes no output. Base-kind
# ensconce captures exactly one base, so exactly one slot is populated — the
# single-form facts are one-per-dispatch and buf_write_fact_single's no-clobber
# guard turns any second populated slot into a loud failure. The provenance
# envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR (rbgjl02 pushed it under
# :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the touchmark a consumer needs.
zrbld_ensconce_extract() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"

  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}output.json"
  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"

  buc_log_args "Ensconce output:"
  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"

  local z_n=""
  local z_slot_key=""
  local z_stamp_file=""
  local z_stamp=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
    z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_stamp.txt"
    jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.stamp // empty" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to read stamp for ${z_slot_key}"
    z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
    test -n "${z_stamp}" || continue

    buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
    buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"
    buc_success "Ensconced Lode ${z_stamp} — touchmark fact emitted (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE})"
  done
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbld_*)

rbld_ensconce() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (parallel rbi_ld capture)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory declaring the base ORIGIN"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load.
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Resolve the single base ORIGIN. Base-kind ensconce captures one base per
  # Lode per invocation; multi-base vessels are dispatched per base. Every real
  # vessel declares exactly one base slot today.
  local z_origin=""
  local z_origin_count=0
  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_slot_origin=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_slot_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    test -n "${z_slot_origin}" || continue
    z_origin="${z_slot_origin}"
    z_origin_count=$((z_origin_count + 1))
  done

  test "${z_origin_count}" -ne 0 \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slot (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
  test "${z_origin_count}" -eq 1 \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares ${z_origin_count} base slots; base-kind ensconce captures one base per Lode — invoke per base"

  # Reject producer-vessel pins: an origin naming a local vessel directory is a
  # made-side hallmark-pin (bind/airgap), not an upstream base to capture.
  test ! -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}" \
    || buc_die "Origin '${z_origin}' names a producer vessel — base-Lode capture is for upstream bases, not hallmark-pins"

  buc_info "Ensconce base: ${z_origin}"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (skopeo for capture, docker for vouch).
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
  local z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"

  # Tweak override: test infrastructure pins the stamp via the bure tweak channel
  # to drive two captures onto one touchmark (the collision-guard exercise) — the
  # time-based mint is seconds-grained, so two CLI ensconces never share a
  # touchmark and the cloud guard's idempotent/collision branches never fire
  # without a pin. The name carries the buo sprue (BURE enforces the shape).
  # Mirror: rbtdrc_crucible.rs RBTDRC_ENSCONCE_STAMP_TWEAK_NAME — same literal.
  local -r z_ensconce_stamp_tweak_name="buorb_ensconce_stamp"
  test "${BURE_TWEAK_NAME:-}" != "${z_ensconce_stamp_tweak_name}" || z_stamp="${BURE_TWEAK_VALUE}"

  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"

  zrbld_ensconce_submit "${z_token}" "${z_origin}" "${z_stamp}"
  zrbld_ensconce_extract

  buc_success "Ensconce complete: ${z_origin} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
}

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - kindle entry: the single rbld inclusion-guard and kindle,
20	# sourcing the guard-free body clusters (rbldl_ lifecycle, rblds_ capture spine,
21	# rbldb_ bole). The readonly ZRBLD_* constants the kindle sets are read globally
22	# by the clusters.
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	# Multiple inclusion detection
27	test -z "${ZRBLD_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbld multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
28	ZRBLD_SOURCED=1
29	
30	# Source shared Foundry Core module
31	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh"
32	
33	# Lode body clusters (guard-free; sourced once here, the single rbld entry)
34	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldl_Lifecycle.sh"
35	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rblds_Spine.sh"
36	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldb_Bole.sh"
37	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldr_Reliquary.sh"
38	
39	######################################################################
40	# Internal Functions (zrbld_*)
41	
42	zrbld_kindle() {
43	  test -z "${ZRBLD_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbld already kindled"
44	
45	  buc_log_args 'Validate Foundry Core is kindled'
46	  zrbfc_sentinel
47	
48	  buc_log_args 'Verify Director RBRA file'
49	  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
50	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
51	
52	  buc_log_args 'RBGJL ensconce step scripts (same Tools directory)'
53	  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
54	  readonly ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjl"
55	  test -d "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJL steps directory not found: ${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}"
56	
57	  buc_log_args 'Define ensconce operation file prefix'
58	  readonly ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbld_ensconce_"
59	
60	  buc_log_args 'Define conclave operation file prefix'
61	  readonly ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbld_conclave_"
62	
63	  # Google-hosted docker builder — always pullable even under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS.
64	  # Conclave captures the reliquary tool cohort itself, so it cannot resolve its
65	  # builders from a reliquary (the bootstrap it would be creating); both conclave
66	  # steps ride this Google-hosted builder instead of the reliquary-resolved docker.
67	  buc_log_args 'Define Google-hosted docker builder image'
68	  readonly ZRBLD_GOOGLE_DOCKER_BUILDER="gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker"
69	
70	  buc_log_args 'Define divine operation file prefix'
71	  readonly ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbld_divine_"
72	
73	  buc_log_args 'Define banish operation file prefix'
74	  readonly ZRBLD_BANISH_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbld_banish_"
75	
76	  readonly ZRBLD_KINDLED=1
77	}
78	
79	zrbld_sentinel() {
80	  zrbfc_sentinel
81	  test "${ZRBLD_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbld not kindled - call zrbld_kindle first"
82	}
83	
84	# eof
85

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - kindle entry: the single rbld inclusion-guard and kindle,
# sourcing the guard-free body clusters (rbldl_ lifecycle, rblds_ capture spine,
# rbldb_ bole). The readonly ZRBLD_* constants the kindle sets are read globally
# by the clusters.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBLD_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbld multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBLD_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core module
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh"

# Lode body clusters (guard-free; sourced once here, the single rbld entry)
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldl_Lifecycle.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rblds_Spine.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldb_Bole.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldr_Reliquary.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbld_*)

zrbld_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBLD_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbld already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Validate Foundry Core is kindled'
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Verify Director RBRA file'
  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"

  buc_log_args 'RBGJL ensconce step scripts (same Tools directory)'
  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
  readonly ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjl"
  test -d "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJL steps directory not found: ${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}"

  buc_log_args 'Define ensconce operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbld_ensconce_"

  buc_log_args 'Define conclave operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbld_conclave_"

  # Google-hosted docker builder — always pullable even under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS.
  # Conclave captures the reliquary tool cohort itself, so it cannot resolve its
  # builders from a reliquary (the bootstrap it would be creating); both conclave
  # steps ride this Google-hosted builder instead of the reliquary-resolved docker.
  buc_log_args 'Define Google-hosted docker builder image'
  readonly ZRBLD_GOOGLE_DOCKER_BUILDER="gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker"

  buc_log_args 'Define divine operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbld_divine_"

  buc_log_args 'Define banish operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBLD_BANISH_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbld_banish_"

  readonly ZRBLD_KINDLED=1
}

zrbld_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBLD_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbld not kindled - call zrbld_kindle first"
}

# eof

# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"

zrbld_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_NOW_STAMP        " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided string unique between invocations"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBCC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbuh_Http.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbge_Rest.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rba_Auth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbld0_Lode.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbndb_base.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate BUD environment'
  zburd_kindle

  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrd_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbrd_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbuh_kindle
  zrbge_kindle
  zrba_kindle
  zrbfc_kindle
  zrbld_kindle
  zrbndb_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbld_ "Recipe Bottle Lode" zrbld_furnish "$@"

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - lifecycle REST (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
20	#   divine — enumerate Lodes / inspect one Lode's members (read-only)
21	#   banish — delete a whole Lode (Director credentials)
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# External Functions (rbld_*)
27	
28	rbld_divine() {
29	  zrbld_sentinel
30	
31	  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
32	
33	  buc_doc_brief "Divine Lodes — enumerate all Lodes, or inspect one Lode's members (read-only)"
34	  buc_doc_oparm "touchmark" "Lode stamp to inspect (e.g., b260602120000); omit to enumerate all Lodes"
35	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
36	
37	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
38	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
39	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
40	  local z_token=""
41	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
42	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
43	
44	  if test -z "${z_touchmark}"; then
45	    buc_step "Enumerating Lodes under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
46	    zrbfc_list_anchors_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}"
47	
48	    if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
49	      buc_info "No Lodes found under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
50	      buc_success "Divine complete — 0 Lodes"
51	      return 0
52	    fi
53	
54	    # Kind-letter legend, printed once so rows carry no repeated per-row column.
55	    # A touchmark's leading letter is its kind (b260602075327 -> bole); the
56	    # reader decodes the prefix from this key. One entry per implemented kind.
57	    local -r z_kind_fmt="    %-3s %-10s %s\n"
58	    echo ""
59	    printf "  Kinds (touchmark prefix):\n"
60	    printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}"      "bole"      "upstream OCI image, consumed as a FROM line"
61	    printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY}" "reliquary" "date-cohort of build-tool images"
62	
63	    # Load the touchmark list fully before iterating: the per-Lode tags fetch
64	    # spawns curl (via rbuh), and a child touching stdin would consume the
65	    # loop's remaining input. Load-then-iterate keeps that FD closed.
66	    local z_touchmarks=()
67	    local z_touch=""
68	    while IFS= read -r z_touch || test -n "${z_touch}"; do
69	      test -n "${z_touch}" || continue
70	      z_touchmarks+=("${z_touch}")
71	    done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
72	
73	    local -r z_row_fmt="  %-15s %s\n"
74	    echo ""
75	    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "TOUCHMARK" "IMAGE"
76	    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "---------------" "--------------------------------------"
77	
78	    local z_idx=0
79	    local z_pkg=""
80	    local z_pkg_encoded=""
81	    local z_tags_url=""
82	    local z_enum_infix=""
83	    local z_resp_file=""
84	    local z_image_file=""
85	    local z_image=""
86	    for z_idx in "${!z_touchmarks[@]}"; do
87	      z_touch="${z_touchmarks[$z_idx]}"
88	
89	      # One tags-list per Lode. The IMAGE column adapts to the Lode's member
90	      # scheme (the touchmark prefix names the kind; see the legend above). A
91	      # single-image Lode (bole) shows its unsprued fingerprint tag
92	      # <sanitized-origin>-<sha10>, located via the sha10 taken from the
93	      # rbi_sha256-<hex> member tag so Director semantic names (also unsprued)
94	      # cannot masquerade as the fingerprint. A clean-scheme cohort (reliquary)
95	      # carries no digest/fingerprint layer, so it has no single fingerprint to
96	      # show — report its member count instead (every non-:rbi_vouch tag is a
97	      # member). Per-Lode infix preserves each response for forensics.
98	      z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touch}"
99	      z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
100	      z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
101	      z_enum_infix="rbld_divine_enum_${z_idx}"
102	      rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_enum_infix}"
103	      rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touch}" "${z_enum_infix}"
104	      z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_enum_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
105	
106	      z_image_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}enum_${z_idx}_image.txt"
107	      jq -r --arg dp "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" --arg vouch "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" '
108	        [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")] as $names
109	        | ([$names[] | select(startswith($dp)) | ltrimstr($dp)[0:10]][0]) as $sha10
110	        | ([$names[] | select((startswith("rbi_") | not) and ($sha10 != null) and endswith("-" + $sha10))][0]) as $fingerprint
111	        | if $fingerprint != null then $fingerprint
112	          else "(cohort: \([$names[] | select(. != $vouch)] | length) members)"
113	          end
114	      ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_image_file}" \
115	        || buc_die "Failed to summarize Lode ${z_touch}"
116	      z_image=$(<"${z_image_file}")
117	      test -n "${z_image}" || buc_die "Empty summary extraction for Lode ${z_touch}"
118	
119	      printf "${z_row_fmt}" "${z_touch}" "${z_image}"
120	    done
121	    echo ""
122	    buc_info "Total Lodes: ${#z_touchmarks[@]}"
123	    buc_success "Divine complete"
124	    return 0
125	  fi
126	
127	  # Inspect depth: list the member tags on one Lode package.
128	  buc_step "Inspecting Lode ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
129	  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
130	  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
131	  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
132	  local -r z_tags_infix="rbld_divine_tags"
133	
134	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
135	  rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_tags_infix}"
136	
137	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
138	  local -r z_tags_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}tags.txt"
139	  jq -r '.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_tags_file}" \
140	    || buc_die "Failed to extract member tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}"
141	
142	  if ! test -s "${z_tags_file}"; then
143	    buc_die "No member tags found under ${z_pkg} — Lode not present in registry"
144	  fi
145	
146	  echo ""
147	  printf "  %s\n" "MEMBER-TAG"
148	  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"
149	  local z_count=0
150	  local z_tag=""
151	  while IFS= read -r z_tag || test -n "${z_tag}"; do
152	    test -n "${z_tag}" || continue
153	    printf "  %s\n" "${z_tag}"
154	    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
155	  done < "${z_tags_file}"
156	  echo ""
157	  buc_info "Total members: ${z_count}"
158	  buc_success "Divine complete — Lode ${z_touchmark}"
159	}
160	
161	rbld_banish() {
162	  zrbld_sentinel
163	
164	  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
165	
166	  buc_doc_brief "Banish a Lode — delete the whole rbi_ld/<touchmark> GAR package"
167	  buc_doc_param "touchmark" "Lode stamp to delete (e.g., b260602120000)"
168	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
169	
170	  test -n "${z_touchmark}" || buc_die "Touchmark parameter required"
171	
172	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
173	  local z_token=""
174	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
175	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
176	
177	  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
178	  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
179	
180	  # Verify presence before delete so banish reports a clean not-found.
181	  buc_step "Verifying Lode present: ${z_pkg}"
182	  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1"
183	  local -r z_probe_infix="rbld_banish_probe"
184	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_probe_infix}"
185	  rbuh_require_ok "Probe Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_probe_infix}"
186	
187	  local z_tag_count=""
188	  z_tag_count=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_probe_infix}" '(.tags // []) | length') \
189	    || buc_die "Failed to count tags for ${z_pkg}"
190	  test "${z_tag_count}" -gt 0 \
191	    || buc_die "No Lode found at ${z_pkg} — nothing to banish"
192	
193	  buc_require "Will banish the whole Lode ${z_pkg} (single packages delete)" "yes"
194	
195	  # Single packages delete removes the package and all its member versions/tags
196	  # atomically. DELETE returns a long-running operation; trust 200 as accepted.
197	  buc_step "Deleting Lode package: ${z_pkg}"
198	  local -r z_del_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}"
199	  local -r z_del_infix="rbld_banish_del"
200	  rbuh_json "DELETE" "${z_del_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_del_infix}"
201	  rbuh_require_ok "Delete Lode package ${z_pkg}" "${z_del_infix}"
202	
203	  echo ""
204	  buc_success "Lode banished: ${z_touchmark}"
205	}
206	
207	# eof
208

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - lifecycle REST (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
#   divine — enumerate Lodes / inspect one Lode's members (read-only)
#   banish — delete a whole Lode (Director credentials)

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbld_*)

rbld_divine() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Divine Lodes — enumerate all Lodes, or inspect one Lode's members (read-only)"
  buc_doc_oparm "touchmark" "Lode stamp to inspect (e.g., b260602120000); omit to enumerate all Lodes"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  if test -z "${z_touchmark}"; then
    buc_step "Enumerating Lodes under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
    zrbfc_list_anchors_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}"

    if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
      buc_info "No Lodes found under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
      buc_success "Divine complete — 0 Lodes"
      return 0
    fi

    # Kind-letter legend, printed once so rows carry no repeated per-row column.
    # A touchmark's leading letter is its kind (b260602075327 -> bole); the
    # reader decodes the prefix from this key. One entry per implemented kind.
    local -r z_kind_fmt="    %-3s %-10s %s\n"
    echo ""
    printf "  Kinds (touchmark prefix):\n"
    printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}"      "bole"      "upstream OCI image, consumed as a FROM line"
    printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY}" "reliquary" "date-cohort of build-tool images"

    # Load the touchmark list fully before iterating: the per-Lode tags fetch
    # spawns curl (via rbuh), and a child touching stdin would consume the
    # loop's remaining input. Load-then-iterate keeps that FD closed.
    local z_touchmarks=()
    local z_touch=""
    while IFS= read -r z_touch || test -n "${z_touch}"; do
      test -n "${z_touch}" || continue
      z_touchmarks+=("${z_touch}")
    done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"

    local -r z_row_fmt="  %-15s %s\n"
    echo ""
    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "TOUCHMARK" "IMAGE"
    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "---------------" "--------------------------------------"

    local z_idx=0
    local z_pkg=""
    local z_pkg_encoded=""
    local z_tags_url=""
    local z_enum_infix=""
    local z_resp_file=""
    local z_image_file=""
    local z_image=""
    for z_idx in "${!z_touchmarks[@]}"; do
      z_touch="${z_touchmarks[$z_idx]}"

      # One tags-list per Lode. The IMAGE column adapts to the Lode's member
      # scheme (the touchmark prefix names the kind; see the legend above). A
      # single-image Lode (bole) shows its unsprued fingerprint tag
      # <sanitized-origin>-<sha10>, located via the sha10 taken from the
      # rbi_sha256-<hex> member tag so Director semantic names (also unsprued)
      # cannot masquerade as the fingerprint. A clean-scheme cohort (reliquary)
      # carries no digest/fingerprint layer, so it has no single fingerprint to
      # show — report its member count instead (every non-:rbi_vouch tag is a
      # member). Per-Lode infix preserves each response for forensics.
      z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touch}"
      z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
      z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
      z_enum_infix="rbld_divine_enum_${z_idx}"
      rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_enum_infix}"
      rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touch}" "${z_enum_infix}"
      z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_enum_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"

      z_image_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}enum_${z_idx}_image.txt"
      jq -r --arg dp "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" --arg vouch "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" '
        [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")] as $names
        | ([$names[] | select(startswith($dp)) | ltrimstr($dp)[0:10]][0]) as $sha10
        | ([$names[] | select((startswith("rbi_") | not) and ($sha10 != null) and endswith("-" + $sha10))][0]) as $fingerprint
        | if $fingerprint != null then $fingerprint
          else "(cohort: \([$names[] | select(. != $vouch)] | length) members)"
          end
      ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_image_file}" \
        || buc_die "Failed to summarize Lode ${z_touch}"
      z_image=$(<"${z_image_file}")
      test -n "${z_image}" || buc_die "Empty summary extraction for Lode ${z_touch}"

      printf "${z_row_fmt}" "${z_touch}" "${z_image}"
    done
    echo ""
    buc_info "Total Lodes: ${#z_touchmarks[@]}"
    buc_success "Divine complete"
    return 0
  fi

  # Inspect depth: list the member tags on one Lode package.
  buc_step "Inspecting Lode ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
  local -r z_tags_infix="rbld_divine_tags"

  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
  rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_tags_infix}"

  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  local -r z_tags_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}tags.txt"
  jq -r '.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_tags_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract member tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}"

  if ! test -s "${z_tags_file}"; then
    buc_die "No member tags found under ${z_pkg} — Lode not present in registry"
  fi

  echo ""
  printf "  %s\n" "MEMBER-TAG"
  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"
  local z_count=0
  local z_tag=""
  while IFS= read -r z_tag || test -n "${z_tag}"; do
    test -n "${z_tag}" || continue
    printf "  %s\n" "${z_tag}"
    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
  done < "${z_tags_file}"
  echo ""
  buc_info "Total members: ${z_count}"
  buc_success "Divine complete — Lode ${z_touchmark}"
}

rbld_banish() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Banish a Lode — delete the whole rbi_ld/<touchmark> GAR package"
  buc_doc_param "touchmark" "Lode stamp to delete (e.g., b260602120000)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_touchmark}" || buc_die "Touchmark parameter required"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"

  # Verify presence before delete so banish reports a clean not-found.
  buc_step "Verifying Lode present: ${z_pkg}"
  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1"
  local -r z_probe_infix="rbld_banish_probe"
  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_probe_infix}"
  rbuh_require_ok "Probe Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_probe_infix}"

  local z_tag_count=""
  z_tag_count=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_probe_infix}" '(.tags // []) | length') \
    || buc_die "Failed to count tags for ${z_pkg}"
  test "${z_tag_count}" -gt 0 \
    || buc_die "No Lode found at ${z_pkg} — nothing to banish"

  buc_require "Will banish the whole Lode ${z_pkg} (single packages delete)" "yes"

  # Single packages delete removes the package and all its member versions/tags
  # atomically. DELETE returns a long-running operation; trust 200 as accepted.
  buc_step "Deleting Lode package: ${z_pkg}"
  local -r z_del_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}"
  local -r z_del_infix="rbld_banish_del"
  rbuh_json "DELETE" "${z_del_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_del_infix}"
  rbuh_require_ok "Delete Lode package ${z_pkg}" "${z_del_infix}"

  echo ""
  buc_success "Lode banished: ${z_touchmark}"
}

# eof

content.content:
=== buildx-push ===
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS buildx-push — push a prepared FROM-scratch build context as an image via
# the shared buildx builder (see buildx-bootstrap). The irreducible push shared
# by the FROM-scratch vouch-push callers (Lode rbgjl02, hallmark-verify rbgjv03):
# push cardinality, the destination URI, the platform set, and the context
# assembly (which JSON, conditional Dockerfile) are all done by the kind.
#   requires: PUSH_URI        full destination image ref including tag
#             PUSH_PLATFORMS  buildx --platform value (e.g. linux/amd64)
#             PUSH_CTX        build context dir holding the Dockerfile
#   provides: the image pushed to PUSH_URI
docker buildx build \
  --push \
  --platform="${PUSH_PLATFORMS}" \
  --tag "${PUSH_URI}" \
  "${PUSH_CTX}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: buildx push failed for ${PUSH_URI}" >&2; exit 1; }

=== buildx-bootstrap ===
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS buildx-bootstrap — ensure the shared docker-container buildx builder
# exists and is selected. Idempotent under Cloud Build retry: inspect-or-create,
# then use. Run once per step before any push; safe to re-run (a second inspect
# succeeds, create is skipped, use is a no-op).
#   requires: (none)
#   provides: the "rb-builder" buildx builder, created if absent and selected
docker buildx inspect rb-builder >/dev/null 2>&1 \
  || docker buildx create --driver docker-container --name rb-builder
docker buildx use rb-builder

=== token-fetch ===
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS token-fetch — OAuth2 access token from the Cloud Build metadata server
# (Mason SA). The canonical skopeo-to-GAR authentication (RBSCB): skopeo cannot
# use the gcloud credential helper, so the token is fetched in-memory and never
# written to /workspace, which carries non-secret data only.
#   requires: (none — ambient metadata server)
#   provides: TOKEN
echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }

=== skopeo-fingerprint ===
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS skopeo-fingerprint — inspect an upstream image's raw manifest, take its
# canonical sha256 digest, and derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
# The canonical digest is the sha256 of the RAW manifest bytes (what every tool
# reports); the fingerprint is the sanitized origin (`:` and `/` become `-`) plus
# the first 10 hex of that digest — the legacy enshrine anchor form. Only this
# inspect+digest+fingerprint core is identical across the skopeo captures
# (ensconce, enshrine); the first GAR copy is kind-specific (its destination tag
# is itself digest-derived) and stays in the step.
#   requires: ORIGIN    upstream image ref (e.g. docker.io/library/x:tag)
#             RAW_FILE  /workspace path to write the raw manifest into
#   provides: SHA          64-hex canonical digest (no algorithm prefix)
#             FINGERPRINT  "<sanitized-origin>-<first-10-hex>"
skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

# sha256sum (coreutils, not openssl) — runs inside the skopeo reliquary container.
SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

# Sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars (legacy anchor form).
FINGERPRINT="$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')-${SHA:0:10}"

content.text: The kind-letter `w` already exists. Now let me read the conclave spec (RBSLU template) and check builder-image / tarball-fetch precedent across cloud steps before I design the new capture step.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_lode_conclave}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation captures the build-tool cohort into a single
5	{rbtga_lode}
6	— one
7	{gar_service}
8	package under the
9	{gar_lodes_namespace}
10	namespace, identified by a host-minted
11	{rbtga_touchmark}.
12	It is the reliquary kind: the co-versioned set of Cloud Build tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo) consumed as a build toolset.
13	It absorbs the legacy inscribe tool-mirror — retargeted from the per-tool sibling-package layout to N member tags within one Lode package, plus the unifying
14	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
15	envelope.
16	
17	NOTE: Acquisition runs cloud-side.
18	The
19	{rbtr_director}
20	submits a
21	{gcb_service}
22	job on the tethered (egress-permitted) pool, executed by the
23	{rbtr_mason}
24	service account via the metadata server.
25	The workstation never fetches the upstream bytes — it trusts the GCP environment over a potentially-compromised station.
26	
27	NOTE: Conclave bootstraps from no reliquary.
28	The tool images captured here ARE a reliquary, so capture cannot resolve its builders from one.
29	Both steps ride the Google-hosted `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` builder (always pullable), and the
30	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
31	envelope — not the acquisition tool — is the unifier, so a docker-pull path alongside the bole kind's skopeo capture is correct.
32	
33	NOTE: Conclave is capture-pure.
34	It mints the Lode, emits the
35	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
36	provenance envelope, and reports the touchmark — but writes NO consumer config.
37	Election (where a vessel pins the reliquary touchmark) is a separate consumption-side yoke-stamp layer that reads the
38	{rbf_fact_lode}
39	this operation writes; it is not part of conclave.
40	
41	The
42	{rbtr_director}
43	convenes a reliquary cohort into a Lode:
44	
45	//axhos_step
46	. *Authenticate as
47	{rbtr_director}*:
48	.. Execute
49	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
50	.. {rbbc_store}
51	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Mint Touchmark (host-side)*:
55	.. {rbbc_store}
56	`«TOUCHMARK»` as the reliquary kind-letter `r` + `YYMMDDHHMMSS` from current time
57	.. The host owns the stamp so the touchmark — and thus the
58	{rbf_fact_lode}
59	path — is known before the build
60	.. Example: `r260602120000`
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Submit Two-Step Capture Cloud Build*:
64	.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
65	... Step 1 — the Google-hosted docker builder image, script `rbgjl03-conclave-capture.sh` (the cohort capture step)
66	... Step 2 — the Google-hosted docker builder image, script `rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (the vouch push)
67	... substitutions carrying GAR host/path, the
68	{gar_lodes_namespace}
69	root, `«TOUCHMARK»`, the member-tag sprue, the acquiring SA email, the trust grade, and the envelope schema
70	... serviceAccount:
71	{rbtr_mason}
72	... pool: tethered (upstream pull requires public egress)
73	.. {rbbc_submit}
74	link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/create[builds.create] with:
75	... project:
76	{rbtgi_depot_project}
77	... location:
78	{rbrd_gcp_region}
79	.. {rbbc_store}
80	`«BUILD_ID»` from response `.metadata.build.id`
81	.. {rbbc_show}
82	build dispatched with `«BUILD_ID»` and the Cloud Console URL
83	
84	//axhos_step
85	. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl03)*:
86	.. For each tool in the co-versioned cohort manifest (`<short-name>|<upstream-ref>`):
87	... Pull the upstream image (`docker pull`) and record its `RepoDigests` digest for the envelope
88	... Tag and push it into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_<tool>` (`docker tag` / `docker push`) — a distinct manifest per tool, all sharing the one package; no digest or fingerprint layer
89	.. Author the
90	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
91	envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, trust grade `verified-against-published`, `members[]` one element per tool — the cardinality axis) and stage it on the {rbtgr_workspace}
92	.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`
93	
94	//axhos_step
95	. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
96	.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package
97	
98	//axhos_step
99	. *Wait for Build Completion*:
100	.. {rbbc_poll}
101	link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/get[cloudbuild.builds.get] for `«BUILD_ID»`
102	.. {rbbc_require}
103	final status is `SUCCESS`
104	
105	//axhoot_typed_output
106	{rbf_fact_lode}
107	. *Write Capture-Facts*:
108	.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` (base64-decoded JSON) from the completed build to recover the host-minted `«TOUCHMARK»`
109	.. {rbbc_store}
110	the touchmark value and the reliquary kind-brand enum as the two bare single-form facts of the
111	{rbf_fact_lode}
112	— carrying no provenance content; the envelope is the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag, written cloud-side by the vouch step
113	
114	//axhoc_completion
115	Successful execution captures the build-tool cohort into a single
116	{gar_service}
117	package at `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`, carrying each tool manifest under its `:rbi_<tool>` member tag plus the `:rbi_vouch`
118	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
119	envelope, and hands the touchmark forward host-side as the
120	{rbf_fact_lode}.
121	+
122	The operation is capture-pure: no vessel or nameplate config is written.
123	A vessel elects the reliquary touchmark separately by reading the capture-file — so election retries independently and survives, and a one-to-many fan-out across vessels iterates by reading the file rather than by a verb taking N vessel arguments.
124	+
125	Compound provenance is batch-level (the cohort as a whole carries one
126	{rbtga_lode_vouch});
127	members stay individually manipulable for post-bug cleanup but need not each carry independent attestation.
128	The legacy inscribe tool-mirror remains live during the reliquary cutover; conclave does not retire it.
129

lastPrompt: proceed now

2143:{rbtga_touchmark},
2149:One Lode is exactly one GAR package, and that package IS the atomic-delete unit: a single `packages delete` removes the Lode and every member version and tag in one call (see {rbtgo_lode_banish}).
2161:* `:rbi_vouch` — the one-per-Lode {rbtga_lode_vouch} provenance envelope

2170:{rbtga_touchmark}::
2172:{rbtga_lode}
2185:[[rbtga_lode_vouch]]
2187:{rbtga_lode_vouch}::
2189:{rbtga_lode}
2195:It has one canonical home: the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag (the Lode self-describing in the registry, decoded by {rbtgo_lode_augur}). The host-side {rbf_fact_lode} carries only the bare touchmark, not the envelope — so reading host-side provenance costs a GAR call, the accepted price of a single canonical home.
2246:The host-side capture handoff written by {rbtgo_lode_ensconce}: two bare single-form chaining facts — the touchmark value and the kind-brand enum — carrying no provenance content (the envelope lives only in GAR as the {rbtga_lode_vouch} `:rbi_vouch` tag).
3879:Datestamp identifying a {rbtga_lode} — the GAR package holding a captured upstream artifact and its provenance.
3882:Mints host-side at {rbtgo_lode_ensconce} time and serves as the GAR path segment (`{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`), so a single value names the whole Lode package.
3883:The fetched-side parallel of {rbst_hallmark}; carried as the concept {rbtga_touchmark}.

content.text: I now have the capture mechanism resolved (FROM-scratch + COPY the opaque tarball + buildx push, mirroring the vouch-push) and confirmed `curl` runs on the Google docker builder (rbgja02 precedent). Let me look at the conclave's registration surface — tabtarget, zipper enrollment, and the theurge fixture — to mirror it for `underpin`.

input.command:
echo "=== rbw-lC tabtarget ===" && cat tt/rbw-lC.DirectorConclavesReliquary.sh
echo ""
echo "=== lode colophons in rbz_zipper ===" && grep -n "rbw-l\|conclave\|reliquary\|ensconce\|underpin\|divine\|banish\|rbld_" Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh
echo ""
echo "=== brand constants block (rbgc 230-255) ===" && sed -n '218,255p' Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh
input.description: Read conclave tabtarget, zipper lode enrollment, brand constants

content.content:
=== rbw-lC tabtarget ===
#!/bin/bash
export BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.rbw_workbench.sh
exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"

=== lode colophons in rbz_zipper ===
80:  buz_enroll RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY     "rbw-dI"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_inscribe"      ""  "Create reliquary: mirror tool images from upstream to GAR"
81:  buz_enroll RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY         "rbw-dY"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_yoke"          "param1"  "Yoke a reliquary stamp into every vessel's rbrv.env (wildcard fan-out)"
83:  # Lode — fetched-side universal capture (rbw-l, UPPER=mutates GAR/cost, lower=read-only)
84:  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_LODE       "rbw-l"   "Lode — Fetched-side universal capture"
86:  buz_enroll RBZ_ENSCONCE_BOLE          "rbw-lE"  "${z_mod}" "rbld_ensconce"      "param1"  "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (capture)"
87:  buz_enroll RBZ_CONCLAVE_RELIQUARY     "rbw-lC"  "${z_mod}" "rbld_conclave"      ""        "Conclave the build-tool cohort into one Lode (capture)"
88:  buz_enroll RBZ_DIVINE_LODES           "rbw-ld"  "${z_mod}" "rbld_divine"        "param1"  "Divine Lodes — enumerate all, or inspect one Lode's members (read-only)"
89:  buz_enroll RBZ_BANISH_LODE            "rbw-lB"  "${z_mod}" "rbld_banish"        "param1"  "Banish a Lode — delete the whole rbi_ld/<touchmark> package"
109:  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_AIRGAP     "rbw-Oda" "${z_mod}" "rbho_director_airgap"      ""  "Airgap Cloud Build — ensconce, conjure base, conjure airgap, charge moriah, compare plumb"
145:  buz_enroll RBZ_REKON_RELIQUARY        "rbw-irr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_rekon_reliquary"       "param1"  "List tool images present under a reliquary stamp's GAR subtree"
147:  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_RELIQUARIES      "rbw-iar" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_audit_reliquaries"     ""  "Audit reliquaries — list all reliquary stamps"
149:  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_RELIQUARY_IMAGE  "rbw-iwr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 "param1"  "Wrest a reliquary tool image from registry"
152:  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_RELIQUARY_IMAGE "rbw-iJr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            "param1"  "Jettison a reliquary tool image from registry"

=== brand constants block (rbgc 230-255) ===
  # Five kinds, not six: the single-tool kind ('t') was dropped as non-load-
  # bearing — it split from reliquary on cardinality alone, has no consumer
  # (tool images are only ever consumed as a co-versioned cohort), and the
  # package layer already carries 1-vs-N for free (RBSL "Why five kinds, not four").
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE="b"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY="r"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_WSL="w"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_WSL="vw"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_NATIVE="vn"

  # Kind-brand enum — the touchmark's kind carried in the host-side single-form
  # chaining fact a derived-pull election reads to resolve the member tag. It is
  # read as its own fact, NOT parsed from the touchmark's kind-letter prefix
  # (the chaining channel is single-form: opaque values, never parsed). Bole and
  # reliquary land today; later kinds add their brand here with their vertical.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE="bole"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY="reliquary"

  # Member/provenance tags. The rbi_ sprue marks strings from RB's domain:
  # RB's authored lexicon (bole, vouch) and RB-measured-from-content values
  # (the digest). It does NOT mark foreign-cued strings — the sanitized-origin
  # tag is UNSPRUED (origin is a vessel cue), computed at capture, not a constant.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE="rbi_"               # RB reserved tag prefix; member tags compose as <sprue><name>
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE="rbi_bole"            # uniform greppable handle (bole singleton)
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH="rbi_vouch"          # one-per-Lode provenance envelope
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX="rbi_sha256-"  # canonical OCI digest tag: rbi_sha256-<full-hex>
  # reliquary cohort members carry the clean scheme :<sprue><tool> (e.g. rbi_skopeo)
  # — no digest/fingerprint layer; the tool name is RB-authored lexicon, so sprued.

  # Provenance envelope (:rbi_vouch) — two honest trust grades, declared per Lode.
  # bole captures the durable-upstream grade; podvm-* will carry the recorded grade.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED="verified-against-published"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED="recorded-at-acquisition"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA="rbld-vouch-1"    # near-term unsigned, schema-versioned

  # rbi_df layout: flat namespace. No subdirs. Each filename names one
  # depot-scoped artifact; tag varies by artifact role.
  #

=== brand constants block (rbgc 230-255) ===
  # Five kinds, not six: the single-tool kind ('t') was dropped as non-load-
  # bearing — it split from reliquary on cardinality alone, has no consumer
  # (tool images are only ever consumed as a co-versioned cohort), and the
  # package layer already carries 1-vs-N for free (RBSL "Why five kinds, not four").
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE="b"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY="r"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_WSL="w"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_WSL="vw"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_NATIVE="vn"

  # Kind-brand enum — the touchmark's kind carried in the host-side single-form
  # chaining fact a derived-pull election reads to resolve the member tag. It is
  # read as its own fact, NOT parsed from the touchmark's kind-letter prefix
  # (the chaining channel is single-form: opaque values, never parsed). Bole and
  # reliquary land today; later kinds add their brand here with their vertical.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE="bole"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY="reliquary"

  # Member/provenance tags. The rbi_ sprue marks strings from RB's domain:
  # RB's authored lexicon (bole, vouch) and RB-measured-from-content values
  # (the digest). It does NOT mark foreign-cued strings — the sanitized-origin
  # tag is UNSPRUED (origin is a vessel cue), computed at capture, not a constant.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE="rbi_"               # RB reserved tag prefix; member tags compose as <sprue><name>
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE="rbi_bole"            # uniform greppable handle (bole singleton)
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH="rbi_vouch"          # one-per-Lode provenance envelope
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX="rbi_sha256-"  # canonical OCI digest tag: rbi_sha256-<full-hex>
  # reliquary cohort members carry the clean scheme :<sprue><tool> (e.g. rbi_skopeo)
  # — no digest/fingerprint layer; the tool name is RB-authored lexicon, so sprued.

  # Provenance envelope (:rbi_vouch) — two honest trust grades, declared per Lode.
  # bole captures the durable-upstream grade; podvm-* will carry the recorded grade.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED="verified-against-published"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED="recorded-at-acquisition"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA="rbld-vouch-1"    # near-term unsigned, schema-versioned

  # rbi_df layout: flat namespace. No subdirs. Each filename names one
  # depot-scoped artifact; tag varies by artifact role.
  #
